import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut cur = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    let bopt = string.byte_at(s, i)
    match bopt
    when Some(c)
      if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 10 or c == 13
        if has
          result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
          cur = 0
          sign = 1
          has = false
        end
      else if c == 45
        sign = 0 - 1
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (c - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    result = list.push(result, cur * sign)
  end
  result
end

fn bsearch(_ xs: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len() - 1
  while lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = xs.get(mid)
    match v
    when Some(x)
      if x == t
        return Some(mid)
      else if x < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      return None
    end
  end
  None
end

fn run(_ s1: String, _ s2: String) -> Unit
  let xs = parse_ints(s1)
  let topt = string.parse_int(string.trim(s2))
  match topt
  when Some(t)
    match bsearch(xs, t)
    when Some(i)
      println("index=#{i}")
    when None
      println("not_found")
    end
  when None
    println("not_found")
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = io.read_line()
  let line2 = io.read_line()
  match line1
  when Some(s1)
    match line2
    when Some(s2)
      run(s1, s2)
    when None
      ()
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
end
